title: Box Office and Attendance
wikitext:
[]: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by Zeta_One » Wed Jan 02, 2019 2:37 am Does anyone know how many tickets these films sold? I'm only able to find the attendance for opening weekends and total grosses ($18.8 million, $43 million, and $66.4 million). I would like to get a better idea about they performed. Gross isn't a very reliable means to compare the success of films released in different years due to inflation, varying ticket prices, and other factors. For comparison's sake, Shin Godzilla (2016) had attendance figures of 5.69 million and grossed somewhere between $77 million to $80 million (depending on your source) during its Japanese theatrical run.

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by airman4 » Thu Jan 03, 2019 12:12 pm Hum, maybe not the best link yet but try always this https://www.boxofficemojo.com/franchise ... htm&p=.htm Rank Title (click to view) Studio Worldwide Domestic / % Overseas / % Year 1 Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo Elev. $60.5 $175k 0.3% $60.3 99.7% 2014 2 Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance Elev. $41.2 $134k 0.3% $41.0 99.7% 2011 3 Evangelion 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone Elev. $224k $108k 48.2% $116k 51.8% 2009

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Jan 03, 2019 5:40 pm Final is probably going to surpass what 3.0 did as well. Though I think Makoto Shinkai's next film, Whethering With You , is going to have such a large audience carry over from his past film, Your Name , that it should easily set an opening day/weekend attendance record in Japan. Probably one that won't be beat for years.... or maybe until Hayao Miyazaki's last Last LAST film gets released.

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by Zeta_One » Tue Feb 26, 2019 1:50 pm ¥2.00 billion ($18.0 million) / 1.50 million admissions - Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone (2007) ¥4.00 billion ($44.0 million) / 2.90 million admissions - Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance (2009) ¥5.30 billion ($64.0 million) / 3.85 million admissions - Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Redo (2012)

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Feb 26, 2019 8:48 pm ∆ Awesome! Where'd you find it?

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Feb 27, 2019 3:29 am In the respective Wikipedia articles, I presume. EDIT: I thought pointing it out was enough, it's kind of baffling that posting seems to be less effort than doing a 2 second Google search. here

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Feb 27, 2019 10:24 am I don't think that's listed there. I frequent wikipedia (fuckin nerd lol). Edit: I should have clarified, I mean tickets sale data specifically. I'm not seeing that on the wikipedia page. Going to wikipedia is no effort, but it does consume the data I need on my 2-hour travel lel.

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by Zeta_One » Sat Mar 02, 2019 4:47 am Yeah. Wikipedia doesn't have the admission numbers. But I got them by request from a box office forum.

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by robersora » Sat Mar 02, 2019 8:48 am My bad. I apologize.

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Mar 05, 2019 12:35 pm ∆ Don't worry bro. I was recently visiting topics from the 3.0 marketing days. It's crazy how the first proper trailer didn't come out till one month before the film. And it was like 20 seconds of footage. But that month was hype enough to make 3.0 the most succesful Rebuild.

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Mar 05, 2019 2:32 pm Forever one of the most amazing things about 3.0 is how no one across the globe had the slightest idea what the movie actually was until it came out. Everyone figured it was going to be a pretty direct adaptation of Episodes 20 to 24. Nope.

[]: Re: Box Office and Attendance             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Mar 05, 2019 4:38 pm Shows you can fill cinemas without revealing much beforehand.
